---
title: "Canada Data Observatory"
subtitle: "Interactive metrics for informed civic conversation"
---

Canada Data Observatory provides interactive, regularly updated charts and downloadable data on Canada's key metrics. All data comes from official sources — primarily [Statistics Canada](https://www.statcan.gc.ca/) and the [OECD](https://data.oecd.org/) — and is updated automatically each week.

Where possible, Canada is shown alongside a broad set of 20 OECD peer countries (not just the G7) to provide meaningful international context.

This is an open, non-partisan project. It presents data without policy advocacy. Data files are freely downloadable for anyone to use — students, journalists, researchers, and anyone who wants accessible Canadian data.

---

## Topics

::::{.columns}

:::{.column width="33%"}
### [Population](population/)
How many people live in Canada? How has the population changed, and why? National and provincial quarterly estimates.

*Source: Statistics Canada*
:::

:::{.column width="33%"}
### [Economics](economics/)
GDP per capita compared to peers. Inflation trends and the Bank of Canada's 2% target. How does Canada's economy measure up?

*Sources: Statistics Canada, OECD*
:::

:::{.column width="33%"}
### [Science & Innovation](science/)
How much does Canada invest in R&D? How does this compare to peer countries? A key measure of long-term competitiveness.

*Source: OECD MSTI*
:::

::::

---

## Principles

- **Official sources only** — Statistics Canada, OECD, and other authoritative agencies
- **Broad peer comparisons** — 20 OECD countries, not just the G7
- **Automatically updated** — data pipelines run weekly
- **Open and downloadable** — all cleaned data available as CSV
- **No policy advocacy** — the data speaks for itself
